a few highlights from the ampersend Marketplace that went live last week:

@coingecko covers token prices and market data across thousands of assets. @nansen_ai surfaces smart money flows across 500m+ labeled wallets. @Quicknode exposes blockchain endpoints spanning 130+ networks from one api. @ExaAILabs powers ai-native web search built for content retrieval at scale. @pinatacloud handles account-free ipfs storage and retrieval for any agent. Stripe Climate routes agent contributions toward permanent carbon removal projects.

each service charges per request in $usdc and sits one prompt away from any agent that's set up with a wallet. the agent economy starts getting interesting once an agent can ask for any of these services by name and pay for the answer in the same breath.

browse the ampersend Marketplace ⬇️

< The approach of the TASKMARKET protocol >

Daydreams is an AI product lab with a thesis on agentic commerce, that led us to create TASKMARKET.

We've shipped a stack of agentic products. Agent frameworks. USDC inference routers. A popular x402 payment facilitator. A commerce harness for autonomous agents. An agent email layer. A V1 of a task market primitive on Base.

We sit among the most experienced, hands-on practitioners of agentic commerce leveraging all bleeding edge tech from harnesses to stablecoins.

With that in mind we have developed a protocol for agent work and its market layer. We have a full thesis we can't wait to share with you. We intend to ship it with the product.

<Today you can look at the publicly visible contracts of the TASKMARKET.>

The task market protocol is enshrined in Ethereum standards. ERC-8194 (PGTR) and ERC-8195 (TMP). We authored both. They're public on Ethereum.

_ERC-8194 lets agents authorise on-chain action through payment receipts rather than private keys.

_ERC-8195 standardises task markets: escrowed bounties, multiple procurement modes, deterministic task IDs, deliverable hashes anchored on-chain.

Together they're the foundation of TASKMARKET <TMV2>.

This weekend the protocol was hardened and new features added after meaningful community review on the Ethereum Magicians forum.

A community engineer, @0xlamps, read the spec carefully and surfaced three concrete issues. @smooothoor has integrated all three into the protocol.

<The biggest is a hook system>
Same pattern as Uniswap V4. Lifecycle events on the contract that anything can attach to. Before task submission. After task completion. Around acceptance.

What that unlocks is composability. Other protocols can plug into task lifecycle events and build incentive layers, reputation systems, validation, alternative settlement on top of the same substrate.

The task market becomes a shared compute substrate. Everything else can be hooks.

The task market thesis @lordOfAFew has been writing on, where work decomposes into tasks and moves to machine speed, is reaching reality.

A market for verified outcomes is coming into being. It now sits in hardened standards on Ethereum.

TASKMARKET <TMV2> ASSEMBLING
Read the full spec and the community discussion that drove the latest iteration:\n\nhttps://t.co/fPta5PjstZ

$CLAW (Solana) is showing significant relative strength, effectively ignoring the broader macro turbulence affecting the rest of the market.

Technical Analysis:

• Current Price: $0.001356
• Trend: Cautiously bullish. After a capitulation wick to $0.00102, the chart is printing higher lows and higher highs on shorter timeframes.
• Resistance: Immediate ceiling at $0.00137. A clean break above $0.00140 opens up the "air" toward previous highs that you noted.
• Support: Strong floor established at $0.00126, with deeper liquidity at $0.00115.
• Momentum: Volume spikes during the recent dip suggest a "flush out" of weak hands, followed by steady accumulation.

Market Context:

While BTC and major assets are facing headwinds from geopolitical tensions and CLARITY Act reactions, CLAW is outperforming on a relative basis. The consolidation between $0.00126 and $0.00137 suggests the market is digesting the recent move before attempting the next leg up.

Strategy:

• Accumulation: Limit orders in the $0.00126 – $0.00128 zone offer the best risk/reward for entries.
• Profit Taking: Scale out near the $0.00137 – $0.00140 resistance levels unless volume confirms a breakout.
• Risk: Bias remains optimistic as long as $0.00126 holds. A break below that level would invalidate the current bullish structure.

@signulll New OS means new hardware / ecosystem. Dubious you would get enough consumers to switch before Apple / Google get good enough.

@signulll Without a Mac Mini running, most consumer data is not available to 3rd party apps. Apple and Google have privileged access because of OS (and a lot of important data is in the default services like iMessage and Photos)
